title: SherpaCarta Canada Digital Rights Petition
subtitle: Campaign commitments · paper petitions · official Parliament bridge

jurisdiction.scope: Campaign signatures are Canadian civic commitments. Official Parliamentary and BC Assembly signatures use government or paper channels only.

prayers.campaign: We, residents and citizens of Canada, call on federal and provincial governments to adopt SherpaCarta — the Global Digital Magna Carta — as model legislation for digital human rights, including absolute privacy and prohibition of mass surveillance (Art. 11), full data sovereignty (Art. 12), prohibition of surveillance capitalism (Art. 13), right to be forgotten (Art. 47), algorithmic transparency and non-discrimination (Arts. 61–62), and the living charter principle that rights may only expand (Art. 114). We support modernizing PIPEDA, establishing a BC Digital Rights Act, and requiring Privacy by Design in public-sector and vendor systems serving Canadians.
prayers.federalOfficial: We, the undersigned citizens and residents of Canada, call upon the House of Commons in Parliament assembled to take concrete action to strengthen digital human rights by modernizing federal privacy law (including PIPEDA) to guarantee meaningful consent, data sovereignty for individuals, limits on mass surveillance and commercial surveillance advertising, a practical right to erasure, and enforceable algorithmic transparency and non-discrimination for automated decisions that significantly affect people in Canada; and to examine model language drawn from the SherpaCarta digital rights charter as a reference framework for such reforms.
prayers.bcOfficial: We, the undersigned residents of British Columbia, petition the Legislative Assembly of British Columbia to adopt a BC Digital Rights Act and municipal Safe Harbour standards protecting privacy, data sovereignty, algorithmic transparency, and the principle that digital rights may only expand; and to use the SherpaCarta charter as model language for provincial legislation and public-sector procurement.

petitionText: We, residents and citizens of Canada, call on federal and provincial governments to adopt SherpaCarta — the Global Digital Magna Carta — as model legislation for digital human rights, including absolute privacy and prohibition of mass surveillance (Art. 11), full data sovereignty (Art. 12), prohibition of surveillance capitalism (Art. 13), right to be forgotten (Art. 47), algorithmic transparency and non-discrimination (Arts. 61–62), and the living charter principle that rights may only expand (Art. 114). We support modernizing PIPEDA, establishing a BC Digital Rights Act, and requiring Privacy by Design in public-sector and vendor systems serving Canadians.

eligibility.campaign: Canadian citizen or resident of Canada (self-attested). No minimum age. No government ID required.
eligibility.federalEPetition: Canadian citizen or resident; no minimum age; name, email, province/postal or country, phone; email confirmation on Parliament site.
eligibility.federalPaper: Citizen or resident; no minimum age; name, address, original signature; min 25 for certification.
eligibility.bcPaper: Original wet signatures with name and address under petition text; presented by MLA.

federal.name: House of Commons e-Petition
federal.requirements: MP authorization; 5–10 supporters; Clerk certification; 500+ validated signatures; email confirmation on Parliament site
federal.note: Not live until Cam secures MP authorization and e-### is published. Campaign buttons must not claim Parliamentary effect until then.

federalPaper.name: House of Commons paper petition
federalPaper.note: Printable sheets; original signatures; MP presents.

bc.name: BC Legislative Assembly Petition
bc.requirements: Paper format only for presentation; original signatures; MLA presents

api.privacy: Stores receipt hash, method, province, attestation bool, timestamp. Optional email HMAC. No phone, street address, or ID documents.

privacy.officialSeparation: Federal and BC official petitions are separate voluntary steps on government websites or original paper

sheet.hook: Privacy is a birthright — not a product feature.
shortWhy: Your data and private life should not be a product sold by default.
When algorithms score jobs, credit, or speech, you deserve a plain-language explanation.
Federal privacy law must catch up to AI-era power — rights online should only expand.
sheet.qrCaption: Scan to sign online too · Campaign commitment (not a substitute for ink on this sheet)

share.defaultText: I signed the SherpaCarta Canada campaign — 114 articles for digital privacy and data sovereignty. Privacy is a birthright. #CanadaBCChallenge #SherpaCarta

mpOutreach.subject: Request to authorize SherpaCarta digital rights e-petition
mpOutreach.body:
Dear [MP Name],

I am writing to request that you authorize an electronic petition on the House of Commons petitions website concerning modernization of federal privacy and digital rights law, using the SherpaCarta charter as a reference framework.

Authorizing publication does not require you to endorse every view in the petition. It allows Canadians to sign on Parliament's official system.

Draft prayer and briefing: https://sherpacarta.org/canada/official.html
Contact: hello@giveabit.io

Thank you for considering this request.

[Your name]
[City, Province]
